causal_chain.to: Increased kinetic energy of water molecules
causal_chain.mechanism: Heat energy is transferred to the water molecules, causing them to move faster.

causal_chain.from: Increased kinetic energy of water molecules
causal_chain.mechanism: Faster moving molecules are more likely to escape the liquid phase and enter the gas phase.

causal_chain.from: Water reaches 100 degrees Celsius at standard atmospheric pressure
causal_chain.to: Vapor pressure of water equals atmospheric pressure
causal_chain.mechanism: Temperature at which the vapor pressure equals the surrounding pressure.

causal_chain.from: Vapor pressure of water equals atmospheric pressure
causal_chain.mechanism: Boiling occurs when the vapor pressure of the liquid equals or exceeds the surrounding atmospheric pressure, allowing bubbles of vapor to form throughout the liquid.

missing_links: Relationship between temperature and kinetic energy of molecules
